author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
Again, on the words of the Gospel, Luke x. 38, etc., about Martha and Mary.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: Again, on the words of the Gospel, Luke x. 38, etc., about Martha and Mary.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the holy Gospel was being read, we heard that the Lord was received by a religious woman into her house, and her name was Martha.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And while she was occupied in the care of serving, her sister Mary was sitting at the Lord’s Feet, and hearing His Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The one was busy, the other was still; one was giving out, the other was being filled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet Martha, all busy as she was in that occupation and toil of serving, appealed to the Lord, and complained of her sister, that she did not help her in her labour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord answered Martha for Mary; and He became her Advocate, who had been appealed to as Judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Martha,” He saith, “thou art occupied about many things, when one thing is necessary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mary hath chosen the better part, which shall not be taken from her.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we have heard both the appeal of the appellant, and the sentence of the Judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Which sentence answered the appellant, defended the other’s cause.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Mary was intent on the sweetness of the Lord’s word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Martha was intent, how she might feed the Lord; Mary intent how she might be fed by the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By Martha a feast was being prepared for the Lord, in whose feast Mary was even now delighting herself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As Mary then was listening with sweet pleasure to His most sweet word, and was feeding with the most earnest affection, when the Lord was appealed to by her sister, how, think we, did she fear, lest the Lord should say to her, “Rise and help thy sister”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For by a wondrous sweetness was she held; a sweetness of the mind which is doubtless greater than that of the senses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She was excused, she sat in greater confidence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how excused?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us consider, examine, investigate it thoroughly as we can, that we may be fed also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what, do we imagine that Martha’s serving was blamed, whom the cares of hospitality had engaged, who had received the Lord Himself into her house?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How could she be rightly blamed, who was gladdened by so great a guest?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If this be true, let men give over their ministrations to the needy; let them choose for themselves “the better part, which shall not be taken from” them; let them give themselves wholly to the word, let them long after the sweetness of doctrine; be occupied about the saving knowledge; let it be no care to them, what stranger is in the street, who there is that wants bread, or clothing, or to be visited, to be redeemed, to be buried; let works of mercy cease, earnest heed be given to knowledge only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If this be “the better part,” why do not all do this, when we have the Lord Himself for our defender in this behalf?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we do not fear in this matter, lest we should offend His justice, when we have the support of His judgment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet it is not so; but as the Lord spake so it is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not as thou understandest; but it is as thou oughtest to understand it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So mark; “Thou art occupied about many things, when one thing is needful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mary hath chosen the better part.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast not chosen a bad part; but she a better.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how better?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because thou art “about many things,” she about “one thing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One is preferred to many.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For one does not come from many, but many from one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The things which were made, are many, He who made them is One.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The heaven, the earth, the sea, and all things that in them are, how many are they!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who could enumerate them? who conceive their vast number?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who made all these?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God made them all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold, “they are very good.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Very good are the things He made; how much better is He who made them!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us consider then our “occupations about many things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Much serving is necessary for the refreshment of our bodies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore is this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because we hunger, and thirst.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mercy is necessary for the miserable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou breakest bread to the hungry; because thou hast found an hungry man; take hunger away; to whom dost thou break bread?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take houseless wandering away; to whom dost thou show hospitality?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take nakedness away; to whom dost thou furnish clothes?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let there be no sickness; whom dost thou visit?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No captivity; whom dost thou redeem?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No quarrelling; whom dost thou reconcile?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No death; whom dost thou bury?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In that world to come, these evils will not be; therefore these services will not be either.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well then did Martha, as touching the bodily—what shall I call it, want, or will, of the Lord?—minister to His mortal flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But who was He in that mortal flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God:” see what Mary was listening to!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us:” see to whom Martha was ministering!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “hath Mary chosen the better part, which shall not be taken from her.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For she chose that which shall abide for ever; “it shall not be taken from her.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She wished to be occupied about “one thing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She understood already, “But it is good for me to cleave to the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She sat at the feet of our Head.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The more lowlily she sat, the more amply did she receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the water flows together to the low hollows of the valley, runs down from the risings of the hill.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord then did not blame Martha’s work, but distinguished between their services.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou art occupied about many things; yet one thing is needful.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Already hath Mary chosen this for herself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The labour of manifoldness passeth away, and the love of unity abideth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore what she hath chosen, “shall not be taken from her.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But from thee, that which thou hast chosen (of course this follows, of course this is understood) from thee, that which thou hast chosen shall be taken away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to thy blessedness shall it be taken away, that that which is better may be given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For labour shall be taken away from thee, that rest may be given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art still on the sea, she is already in port.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see then, dearly Beloved, and, as I suppose, ye understand already, that in these two women, who were both well pleasing to the Lord, both objects of His love, both disciples; ye see, I say (and an important thing it is which whosoever understand, understand hereby, a thing which, even those of you who do not understand ought to give ear to, and to know), that in these two women the two lives are figured, the life present, and the life to come, the life of labour, and the life of quiet, the life of sorrow, and the life of blessedness, the life temporal, and the life eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These are the two lives: do ye think of them more fully.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What this life contains, I speak not of a life of evil, or iniquity, or wickedness, or luxuriousness, or ungodliness; but of labour, and full of sorrows, by fears subdued, by temptations disquieted: even this harmless life I mean, such as was suitable for Martha: this life I say, examine as best ye can; and as I have said, think of it more fully than I speak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But a wicked life was far from that house, and was neither with Martha nor with Mary; and if it ever had been, it fled at the Lord’s entrance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There remained then in that house, which had received the Lord, in the two women the two lives, both harmless, both praiseworthy; the one of labour, the other of ease; neither vicious, neither slothful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both harmless, both, I say, praiseworthy: but one of labour, the other of ease: neither vicious, which the life of labour must beware of; neither slothful, which the life of ease must beware of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There were then in that house these two lives, and Himself, the Fountain of life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In Martha was the image of things present, in Mary of things to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What Martha was doing, that we are now; what Mary was doing, that we hope for.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us do the first well, that we may have the second fully.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what of it have we now?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How far have we it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As long as we are here, how much of it is there that we have?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in some measure are we employed in it now, and ye too when removed from business, and laying aside domestic cares, ye meet together, stand, listen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In so far as ye do this, ye are like Mary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And with greater facility do ye do that which Mary doeth, than I who have to distribute.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet if I say ought, it is Christ’s; therefore doth it feed you, because it is Christ’s.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Bread is common to us all, of which I too live as well as you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But now we live, if ye, Brethren, stand fast in the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would not that ye should stand fast in us, but in the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For neither is he that planteth anything, neither he that watereth; but God that giveth the increase.”
